_description: Published meta-analysis benchmarks for RapidMeta validation. benchmark_type distinguishes external IPD vs aggregate vs self-reference pools; pool_type flags same-drug vs class-level pooling (class-level triggers GRADE indirectness downgrade).

FINERENONE_REVIEW.html.source: Agarwal R et al. Lancet 2022; 400:1788-1801 (FIDELITY pooled FIDELIO-DKD + FIGARO-DKD)
FINERENONE_REVIEW.html.method: pre-specified individual patient data pooled analysis
FINERENONE_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is IPD/patient-level; app uses aggregate-data DL random-effects. Direct point-estimate comparison expected to differ slightly.

BEMPEDOIC_ACID_REVIEW.html.source: Nissen SE et al. N Engl J Med 2023; 388:1353-1364 (CLEAR Outcomes)
BEMPEDOIC_ACID_REVIEW.html.method: single RCT (used as anchor; DL pooled with CLEAR-Harmony gives similar)
BEMPEDOIC_ACID_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

COLCHICINE_CVD_REVIEW.html.source: COLCOT (Tardif JC, N Engl J Med 2019; 381:2497) + LoDoCo2 (Nidorf SM, N Engl J Med 2020; 383:1838)
COLCHICINE_CVD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects pooling of 2 landmark RCTs
COLCHICINE_CVD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

GLP1_CVOT_REVIEW.html.source: Sattar N et al. Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2021; 9:653-662
GLP1_CVOT_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is IPD/patient-level; app uses aggregate-data DL random-effects. Direct point-estimate comparison expected to differ slightly.
GLP1_CVOT_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

SGLT2_HF_REVIEW.html.source: Vaduganathan M et al. Lancet 2022; 400:757-767 (DELIVER + DAPA-HF pooled)
SGLT2_HF_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

PCSK9_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

INTENSIVE_BP_REVIEW.html.source: Internal DL pool: SPRINT-SENIOR (Williamson 2016, HR 0.66) + SPRINT-CKD (Cheung 2017, HR 0.81) + ACCORD-BP (Cushman 2010, HR 0.88) + STEP (Zhang 2021, HR 0.74) + SPS3-BP (Benavente 2013, HR 0.81). External benchmark for comparison: Xie 2016 Lancet HR 0.84 (ACM); Ettehad 2016 Lancet HR 0.87.
INTENSIVE_BP_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects of 5 intensive-BP RCTs/strata. Caveat: SPRINT-SENIOR and SPRINT-CKD are non-independent age/CKD subgroups of parent SPRINT trial; treated as separate strata for subgroup analysis. Q=3.6, df=4, I^2=0%.
INTENSIVE_BP_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
INTENSIVE_BP_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

SGLT2_CKD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects pooling of 3 dedicated CKD trials
SGLT2_CKD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

DOAC_CANCER_VTE_REVIEW.html.method: DL pooling of 4 DOAC-vs-LMWH cancer VTE RCTs

MAVACAMTEN_HCM_REVIEW.html.method: DL pooling of published adjusted odds ratios for NYHA class improvement
MAVACAMTEN_HCM_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

RIVAROXABAN_VASC_REVIEW.html.source: Eikelboom JW (COMPASS, NEJM 2017) + Bonaca MP (VOYAGER-PAD, NEJM 2020) + Zannad F (COMMANDER-HF 2018) + Mega JL (ATLAS-ACS2 2012)
RIVAROXABAN_VASC_REVIEW.html.method: DL pooling of 4 rivaroxaban vascular protection RCTs
RIVAROXABAN_VASC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

LIPID_HUB_REVIEW.html.source: Internal DL pool: REDUCE-IT (Bhatt 2019, HR 0.75) + STRENGTH (Nicholls 2020, HR 1.02) + VITAL (Manson 2019, HR 0.92) + OMEMI (Kalstad 2021, HR 1.08) + RESPECT-EPA (Nishizaki 2024, HR 0.79)
LIPID_HUB_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects of all 5 EPA/omega-3 CVOTs. High heterogeneity (I^2~78%) reflects EPA-only (REDUCE-IT/RESPECT-EPA, beneficial) vs mixed EPA+DHA (STRENGTH/VITAL/OMEMI, neutral). Honest pool crosses null. Earlier 3-trial pool of 0.75 was selection-driven.
LIPID_HUB_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
LIPID_HUB_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

INCRETIN_HFpEF_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Worsening HF events / CV death composite
INCRETIN_HFpEF_REVIEW.html.source: Internal DL pool: STEP-HFpEF (Kosiborod 2023, Peto HR 0.18 from 1/263 vs 12/266 worsening HF) + STEP-HFpEF DM (Kosiborod 2024, Peto HR 0.40 from 7/310 vs 18/306) + SUMMIT (Packer 2024, published HR 0.62 for CV death + HF events composite)
INCRETIN_HFpEF_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects of 3 incretin HFpEF RCTs. STEP HRs Peto-derived from worsening-HF event counts (KCCQ-CSS was primary). SUMMIT contributes published Cox HR of composite primary. Outcome heterogeneity (worsening-HF vs CV-death-composite) flagged. I^2~57%.
INCRETIN_HFpEF_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
INCRETIN_HFpEF_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

ATTR_CM_REVIEW.html.source: DL pool: ATTR-ACT (Maurer 2018, HR 0.70) + ATTRibute-CM (Gillmore 2024, HR 0.72) + HELIOS-B (Fontana 2024, HR 0.67) + APOLLO-B (Berk 2023, Peto HR 0.99 from 10/181 vs 10/179)
ATTR_CM_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects of 4 ATTR-CM RCTs; APOLLO-B contributes Peto-derived HR (mortality was exploratory in APOLLO-B). Q=0.63, df=3, I^2=0%.
ATTR_CM_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
ATTR_CM_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

JAK_UC_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Clinical remission at week 8/10 (induction in moderate-severe UC)
JAK_UC_REVIEW.html.source: Sandborn 2017 NEJM (OCTAVE Induction 1/NCT01465763, tofacitinib) + Danese 2022 Lancet (U-ACHIEVE/NCT02819635, upadacitinib) + Feagan 2021 Lancet (SELECTION Induction A/NCT02914522, filgotinib). Singh 2023 Clin Gastro meta pooled OR ~3.2.
JAK_UC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects OR pool of clinical remission at induction end across 3 phase 3 JAK inhibitor UC RCTs
JAK_UC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
JAK_UC_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

BIOLOGIC_ASTHMA_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Annualized exacerbation rate ratio in severe asthma
BIOLOGIC_ASTHMA_REVIEW.html.source: Ortega 2014 NEJM (MENSA/NCT01691521, mepolizumab) + FitzGerald 2016 Lancet (CALIMA/NCT01914757, benralizumab) + Castro 2018 NEJM (QUEST/NCT02414854, dupilumab) + Menzies-Gow 2021 NEJM (NAVIGATOR/NCT03347279, tezepelumab). Pooled class rate ratio across 4 biologic-target RCTs.
BIOLOGIC_ASTHMA_REVIEW.html.method: Random-effects exacerbation rate ratio pool across 4 severe-asthma biologic trials (anti-IL5, anti-IL5R, anti-IL4R, anti-TSLP)
BIOLOGIC_ASTHMA_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
BIOLOGIC_ASTHMA_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

CFTR_CF_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Absolute ppFEV1 change at 24-48 weeks (placebo-corrected MD)
CFTR_CF_REVIEW.html.source: Ramsey 2011 NEJM (STRIVE-CF/NCT00909220, ivacaftor G551D) + Heijerman 2019 Lancet (VX17-445-102/NCT03525444, ELX/TEZ/IVA F508del homo) + Middleton 2019 NEJM (VX17-445-103/NCT03525548, ELX/TEZ/IVA F508del/MF). Heterogeneous class pool (mono vs triple modulator).
CFTR_CF_REVIEW.html.method: Random-effects MD pool of absolute ppFEV1 change across 3 CFTR-modulator phase 3 RCTs. High I^2 expected given mutation-genotype differences and modulator generation.
CFTR_CF_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
CFTR_CF_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

CGRP_MIGRAINE_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Change in monthly migraine days (placebo-corrected MD)
CGRP_MIGRAINE_REVIEW.html.source: Goadsby 2017 NEJM (STRIVE/NCT02456740, erenumab) + Stauffer 2018 JAMA Neurol (EVOLVE-1/NCT02614183, galcanezumab) + Dodick 2018 JAMA (HALO-EM/NCT02629861, fremanezumab). One pivotal episodic-migraine trial per CGRP mAb.
CGRP_MIGRAINE_REVIEW.html.method: Fixed-effect pool of 4-6 month placebo-corrected MMD reduction (MD) across 3 phase 3 CGRP mAb episodic-migraine RCTs
CGRP_MIGRAINE_REVIEW.html.unit: monthly migraine days change vs placebo
CGRP_MIGRAINE_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is fixed-effect; app uses DL random-effects. In low-I^2 topics the two match closely.
CGRP_MIGRAINE_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

PARP_OVARIAN_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Progression-free survival (1st-line PARP maintenance in ovarian cancer)
PARP_OVARIAN_REVIEW.html.source: Moore 2018 NEJM (SOLO-1/NCT01844986, olaparib BRCA+) + Ray-Coquard 2019 NEJM (PAOLA-1/NCT02477644, olaparib+bevacizumab all-comer) + Gonzalez-Martin 2019 NEJM (PRIMA/NCT02655016, niraparib all-comer). Tew 2020 ASCO Guideline pooled PFS HR ~0.52.
PARP_OVARIAN_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects PFS HR pool across 3 phase 3 PARP maintenance 1st-line ovarian trials. High I^2 expected (SOLO-1 BRCA-restricted vs PAOLA-1/PRIMA all-comer populations).
PARP_OVARIAN_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
PARP_OVARIAN_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

ANTIAMYLOID_AD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: CDR-SB change at 18 months (adjusted mean difference vs placebo)
ANTIAMYLOID_AD_REVIEW.html.source: van Dyck 2023 NEJM (Clarity-AD/NCT03887455) + Sims 2023 JAMA (TRAILBLAZER-ALZ2/NCT04437511) + Budd Haeberlein 2022 JPAD (EMERGE/NCT02484547). Range of published CDR-SB MDs: lecanemab -0.45, donanemab -0.70, aducanumab-high-dose -0.39.
ANTIAMYLOID_AD_REVIEW.html.method: Fixed-effect pool of 18-month CDR-SB change (MD) across three phase-3 anti-amyloid monoclonal antibody trials
ANTIAMYLOID_AD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is fixed-effect; app uses DL random-effects. In low-I^2 topics the two match closely.
ANTIAMYLOID_AD_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

CDK46_MBC_REVIEW.html.source: Finn 2016 NEJM (PALOMA-2/NCT01740427) + Hortobagyi 2016 NEJM (MONALEESA-2/NCT01958021) + Goetz 2017 JCO (MONARCH-3/NCT02246621). Pool of 3 CDK4/6i + AI 1st-line trials.
CDK46_MBC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects pool of PFS HR across 3 phase 3 RCTs of CDK4/6 inhibitors + aromatase inhibitor vs AI alone in HR+/HER2- first-line mBC
CDK46_MBC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
CDK46_MBC_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

ARPI_mHSPC_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Overall survival in metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer
ARPI_mHSPC_REVIEW.html.source: Armstrong 2022 JCO (ARCHES/NCT02677896) + Davis 2019 NEJM (ENZAMET/NCT02446405) + Chi 2019 NEJM (TITAN/NCT02489318) + Smith 2022 NEJM (ARASENS/NCT02799602). Pool of 4 AR pathway inhibitor trials in mHSPC (enzalutamide + apalutamide + darolutamide-triplet).
ARPI_mHSPC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects OS HR pool across 4 mHSPC ARPI phase-3 RCTs. Note: ARCHES/ENZAMET/TITAN are ARPI+ADT vs ADT doublet; ARASENS is ARPI+docetaxel+ADT vs docetaxel+ADT triplet.
ARPI_mHSPC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.
ARPI_mHSPC_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

CANGRELOR_PCI_REVIEW.html.source: Steg PG et al. Lancet 2013;382:1981 — patient-level pooled analysis of CHAMPION-PCI (NCT00305162), CHAMPION-PLATFORM (NCT00385138), and CHAMPION-PHOENIX (NCT01156571). 24,910 patients in original IPD meta; app uses 25,384 across three CT.gov enrollment totals.
CANGRELOR_PCI_REVIEW.html.method: Patient-level IPD pooled OR for 48h primary composite across 3 CHAMPION trials
CANGRELOR_PCI_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is IPD/patient-level; app uses aggregate-data DL random-effects. Direct point-estimate comparison expected to differ slightly.

TAVR_LOWRISK_REVIEW.html.source: Siontis GCM et al. Eur Heart J 2019 — updated MA of low-risk TAVR RCTs including PARTNER 3 (NCT02675114) + Evolut Low Risk (NCT02701283) + NOTION (NCT01057173). Pooled HR for all-cause mortality at 1-2 years.
TAVR_LOWRISK_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool for ACM across 3 TAVR-vs-SAVR RCTs in low-risk patients
TAVR_LOWRISK_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

MITRAL_FUNCMR_REVIEW.html.source: COAPT (Stone NEJM 2018; NCT01626079) + MITRA-FR (Obadia NEJM 2018; NCT01920698) + RESHAPE-HF2 (Anker NEJM 2024; NCT02444338). DL random-effects pool of 3 MitraClip-in-functional-MR RCTs. High heterogeneity (I^2 ~75%) reflects COAPT/RESHAPE-HF2 benefit vs MITRA-FR neutral result.
MITRAL_FUNCMR_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects of composite primary across 3 RCTs
MITRAL_FUNCMR_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark pooling approach documented in the source citation. App uses DL random-effects on aggregate data; small point-estimate differences are methodological, not errors.

INCLISIRAN_REVIEW.html.source: Ray KK et al. NEJM 2020; 382:1507 (ORION-10/11) + Raal FJ et al. NEJM 2020; 382:1520 (ORION-9). Fixed-effect pool across ORION-9/10/11.
INCLISIRAN_REVIEW.html.method: Inverse-variance fixed-effect pool of time-adjusted LDL-C % change, 3 inclisiran phase III RCTs
INCLISIRAN_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is fixed-effect; app uses DL random-effects. In low-I^2 topics the two match closely.
INCLISIRAN_REVIEW.html.surrogate_note: primary outcome is a regulatory surrogate; GRADE indirectness should carry an additional downgrade for the surrogate -> clinical-outcome step

DOAC_AF_REVIEW.html.source: Ruff CT et al. Lancet 2014; 383:955-62 - patient-level IPD meta-analysis of RE-LY (NCT00262600) + ROCKET-AF (NCT00403767) + ARISTOTLE (NCT00412984) + ENGAGE AF-TIMI 48 (NCT00781391)
DOAC_AF_REVIEW.html.method: Patient-level IPD pooled HR for stroke/SE across 4 pivotal DOAC-AF phase 3 RCTs at licensed high-dose arms
DOAC_AF_REVIEW.html.method_note: Published benchmark is IPD/patient-level; app uses aggregate-data DL random-effects. Direct point-estimate comparison expected to differ slightly.
DOAC_AF_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

TIRZEPATIDE_T2D_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: HbA1c change from baseline at primary analysis (%)
TIRZEPATIDE_T2D_REVIEW.html.source: Internal DL random-effects pool of Rosenstock J 2021 Lancet (SURPASS-1/NCT03954834, MD -2.11) + Ludvik B 2021 Lancet (SURPASS-3/NCT03882970, MD -1.04) + Dahl D 2022 JAMA (SURPASS-5/NCT04039503, MD -1.66). Range reflects the mix of placebo-controlled (SURPASS-1, -5) and active-controlled (SURPASS-3 vs insulin degludec) comparisons.
TIRZEPATIDE_T2D_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool of the placebo-/active-corrected HbA1c change at the trial-specific primary analysis timepoint (40-52 wk) across 3 SURPASS trials at the 15 mg top-dose arm. Heterogeneity expected given placebo vs active comparator mix; pooled effect remains clinically substantial.
TIRZEPATIDE_T2D_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool (no single IPD benchmark publication across these exact three trials). External narrative benchmark (Sattar Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2022) cites -1.8 to -2.1% placebo-corrected range for 15 mg across the SURPASS programme; internal pool sits within the expected range.
TIRZEPATIDE_T2D_REVIEW.html.surrogate_note: primary outcome is a regulatory surrogate; GRADE indirectness should carry an additional downgrade for the surrogate -> clinical-outcome step
TIRZEPATIDE_T2D_REVIEW.html.subgroup_prespecified: Pre-specified subgroup analysis on comparator type (placebo vs active / induction vs maintenance / platform-DMT class) is reported alongside the pooled primary estimate.

SEMAGLUTIDE_OBESITY_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Percentage change in body weight at the primary analysis timepoint (68 or 104 weeks)
SEMAGLUTIDE_OBESITY_REVIEW.html.source: Wilding JPH 2021 NEJM (STEP-1/NCT03548935, MD -12.4) + Wadden TA 2021 JAMA (STEP-3/NCT03611582, MD -10.3) + Garvey WT 2022 Nat Med (STEP-5/NCT03693430, MD -12.6). External reference: Singh N et al. 2024 network MA of STEP programme.
SEMAGLUTIDE_OBESITY_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool of placebo-corrected percent body-weight change at the trial-specific primary timepoint (68-104 wk) across the 3 pivotal STEP phase 3 RCTs at the 2.4 mg dose
SEMAGLUTIDE_OBESITY_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; external narrative benchmark (Singh 2024) cites pooled placebo-corrected percent weight-loss MD approximately -12% across STEP phase 3 trials at 2.4 mg.
SEMAGLUTIDE_OBESITY_REVIEW.html.surrogate_note: primary outcome is a regulatory surrogate; GRADE indirectness should carry an additional downgrade for the surrogate -> clinical-outcome step
SEMAGLUTIDE_OBESITY_REVIEW.html.subgroup_prespecified: Pre-specified subgroup analysis on comparator type (placebo vs active / induction vs maintenance / platform-DMT class) is reported alongside the pooled primary estimate.

CART_MM_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Progression-free survival in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma
CART_MM_REVIEW.html.source: San-Miguel J 2023 NEJM (CARTITUDE-4/NCT04181827, cilta-cel, HR 0.26) + Rodriguez-Otero P 2023 NEJM (KarMMa-3/NCT03651128, ide-cel, HR 0.49). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal BCMA-directed CAR-T phase 3 RCTs.
CART_MM_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on the log-hazard scale. k=2 so PI suppressed. Heterogeneity reflects different eligibility (1-3 vs 2-4 prior lines, triple-class-exposed).
CART_MM_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; no single published network MA combining these exact two trials at protocol freeze. Expected HR range 0.26-0.49 reflects patient-population differences (earlier vs later line).
CART_MM_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

ROMOSOZUMAB_OP_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: New vertebral fracture incidence at the trial-specific primary timepoint (12 or 24 months)
ROMOSOZUMAB_OP_REVIEW.html.source: Cosman F 2016 NEJM (FRAME/NCT01575834, RR 0.27) + Saag KG 2017 NEJM (ARCH/NCT01631214, RR 0.52) + Lewiecki EM 2018 JCEM (BRIDGE/NCT02186171, RR 0.06). Bandeira L et al. Osteoporos Int 2022 class-level pool.
ROMOSOZUMAB_OP_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool of new vertebral fracture incidence on log-RR scale across 3 phase 3 romosozumab RCTs. Heterogeneity expected from placebo-controlled (FRAME, BRIDGE) vs active-controlled (ARCH vs alendronate) designs and population (women vs men).
ROMOSOZUMAB_OP_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool aligned with Bandeira 2022 class-level benchmark. Cardiovascular SAE imbalance in ARCH triggered FDA boxed warning (2019); tracked as safety signal.
ROMOSOZUMAB_OP_REVIEW.html.surrogate_note: primary outcome is a regulatory surrogate; GRADE indirectness should carry an additional downgrade for the surrogate -> clinical-outcome step
ROMOSOZUMAB_OP_REVIEW.html.timepoint_harmonisation_note: Pivotal trials report primary outcome at heterogeneous timepoints; primary pool uses shortest common timepoint with longer timepoints as sensitivity.

COPD_TRIPLE_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Moderate or severe exacerbation rate ratio vs dual LAMA/LABA
COPD_TRIPLE_REVIEW.html.source: Lipson DA 2018 NEJM (IMPACT/NCT02164513, RR 0.75) + Rabe KF 2020 NEJM (ETHOS/NCT02465567, RR 0.76) + Ferguson GT 2018 Lancet Resp Med (KRONOS/NCT02497001, RR 0.52 vs ICS/LABA). External benchmark: Calzetta L et al. ERJ Open Res 2022 meta-analysis.
COPD_TRIPLE_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects rate-ratio pool for moderate/severe exacerbation on log-RR scale across 3 phase 3 single-inhaler triple therapy RCTs. KRONOS contributes a comparison vs ICS/LABA (not LAMA/LABA) so heterogeneity partially reflects comparator asymmetry.
COPD_TRIPLE_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; external benchmark (Calzetta 2022) reports RR approximately 0.74 (0.68-0.80) across the same 3 pivotal trials.
COPD_TRIPLE_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

DUPILUMAB_AD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: IGA 0/1 at week 16 in moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis
DUPILUMAB_AD_REVIEW.html.source: Simpson EL 2016 NEJM (SOLO-1/NCT02277743, OR 5.68; SOLO-2/NCT02277769, OR 6.40) + Blauvelt A 2017 Lancet (CHRONOS/NCT02260986, OR 4.64). External benchmark: Sawangjit 2020 JAAD network MA.
DUPILUMAB_AD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects OR pool of IGA 0/1 at week 16 across 3 pivotal dupilumab 300 mg Q2W phase 3 RCTs (SOLO-1 + SOLO-2 monotherapy + CHRONOS with concomitant TCS)
DUPILUMAB_AD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool consistent with Sawangjit 2020 JAAD network MA class-level IGA 0/1 OR ~5-6 vs placebo.

HIGH_EFFICACY_MS_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Annualized relapse rate ratio (high-efficacy anti-CD20 vs platform DMT)
HIGH_EFFICACY_MS_REVIEW.html.source: Hauser SL 2017 NEJM (OPERA-I/NCT01247324, RR 0.54; OPERA-II/NCT01412333, RR 0.53) + Hauser SL 2020 NEJM (ASCLEPIOS-I/NCT02792218, RR 0.49; ASCLEPIOS-II/NCT02792231, RR 0.41).
HIGH_EFFICACY_MS_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects rate-ratio pool on log-RR scale across 4 pivotal anti-CD20 vs platform DMT (IFN-beta-1a or teriflunomide) phase 3 RCTs in RRMS
HIGH_EFFICACY_MS_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; published meta-analyses (Samjoo 2021 J Comp Eff Res NMA) report anti-CD20-class ARR rate ratio consistent with the app pool.
HIGH_EFFICACY_MS_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity
HIGH_EFFICACY_MS_REVIEW.html.subgroup_prespecified: Pre-specified subgroup analysis on comparator type (placebo vs active / induction vs maintenance / platform-DMT class) is reported alongside the pooled primary estimate.

RISANKIZUMAB_CD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at the trial-specific primary timepoint (week 12 induction; week 52 maintenance)
RISANKIZUMAB_CD_REVIEW.html.source: DHaens G 2022 Lancet (ADVANCE/NCT03105128, OR 2.49; MOTIVATE/NCT03104413, OR 3.15) + Ferrante M 2022 Lancet (FORTIFY/NCT03105102, OR 1.79).
RISANKIZUMAB_CD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects OR pool of clinical remission across 2 induction + 1 maintenance phase 3 risankizumab CD RCTs
RISANKIZUMAB_CD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; Singh S et al. 2024 network MA of IBD biologics reports risankizumab-CD clinical-remission OR approximately 2.5-3 vs placebo.
RISANKIZUMAB_CD_REVIEW.html.subgroup_prespecified: Pre-specified subgroup analysis on comparator type (placebo vs active / induction vs maintenance / platform-DMT class) is reported alongside the pooled primary estimate.

RSV_VACCINE_OLDER_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: RSV-associated lower respiratory tract disease (RSV-LRTD) over first RSV season
RSV_VACCINE_OLDER_REVIEW.html.source: Walsh EE 2023 NEJM (RENOIR/NCT05035212, Abrysvo, RR 0.33) + Papi A 2023 NEJM (AReSVi-006/NCT04886596, Arexvy, RR 0.17) + Wilson E 2023 NEJM (ConquerRSV/NCT05127434, mRNA-1345, RR 0.16).
RSV_VACCINE_OLDER_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool of RSV-LRTD on log-RR scale across 3 pivotal phase 3 placebo-controlled RSV vaccine efficacy RCTs in adults >=60
RSV_VACCINE_OLDER_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool corresponds to pooled vaccine efficacy ~80% across licensed products (Abrysvo, Arexvy, mRESVIA). ACIP 2023 noted consistent ~80% VE across the three pivotal trials.
RSV_VACCINE_OLDER_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity
RSV_VACCINE_OLDER_REVIEW.html.timepoint_harmonisation_note: Pivotal trials report primary outcome at heterogeneous timepoints; primary pool uses shortest common timepoint with longer timepoints as sensitivity.

UPADACITINIB_CD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at the trial-specific primary timepoint (wk 12 induction; wk 52 maintenance)
UPADACITINIB_CD_REVIEW.html.source: Loftus EV Jr et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388:1966-1980: U-EXCEL (NCT03345849, OR 2.29) + U-EXCEED (NCT03345836, OR 2.44) + U-ENDURE (NCT03345823, OR 3.77). Internal DL pool of 2 induction + 1 maintenance phase 3 RCTs.
UPADACITINIB_CD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool across induction + maintenance pivotal phase 3 upadacitinib-CD RCTs, computed from 2x2 event counts. Published per-trial adjusted OR values 2.29 (U-EXCEL) + 2.44 (U-EXCEED) + 3.77 (U-ENDURE) are larger than the corresponding RRs because clinical-remission event rates are 14-50%.
UPADACITINIB_CD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool on RR scale (app's native pooler). Published literature reports adjusted OR from logistic regression (pooled ~2.75); Singh S et al. 2024 IBD-biologics NMA reports upadacitinib-CD clinical-remission OR in the expected 2-4 range vs placebo.

FARICIMAB_NAMD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: BCVA letter-score change at week 48 (faricimab minus aflibercept MD)
FARICIMAB_NAMD_REVIEW.html.source: Heier JS et al. Lancet 2022;399:729-740: TENAYA (NCT03823287, ETD +0.7) + LUCERNE (NCT03823300, ETD 0.0). Internal DL pool of 2 replicate phase 3 RCTs.
FARICIMAB_NAMD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on BCVA letter-score scale. Both trials met non-inferiority vs aflibercept with a -4-letter margin; pooled MD ~+0.4 letters is clinically neutral (non-inferiority confirmed, no superiority claim).
FARICIMAB_NAMD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool. TENAYA + LUCERNE were designed as replicate pivotals; FDA and EMA approval based on the two-trial programme.

FEZOLINETANT_VMS_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Change in moderate-to-severe VMS frequency per 24h at week 12 (fezolinetant 45 mg vs placebo, MD)
FEZOLINETANT_VMS_REVIEW.html.source: Lederman S et al. Lancet 2023;401:1091-1102 (SKYLIGHT-1/NCT04003155, MD -1.82) + Johnson KA et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2023;108:1981-1997 (SKYLIGHT-2/NCT04003142, MD -2.55). Internal DL pool of 2 replicate SKYLIGHT phase 3 RCTs.
FEZOLINETANT_VMS_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on VMS-per-day scale across 2 SKYLIGHT pivotal RCTs
FEZOLINETANT_VMS_REVIEW.html.unit: moderate-to-severe VMS per 24h change vs placebo
FEZOLINETANT_VMS_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool. Pooled ETD of ~-2 VMS/day meets the typical MCID for menopausal VMS trials. Safety note: FDA boxed warning for liver-enzyme monitoring; not included as efficacy endpoint.

TDXD_HER2LOW_BC_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Progression-free survival in HER2-low or HR+/HER2-low metastatic breast cancer
TDXD_HER2LOW_BC_REVIEW.html.source: Modi S 2022 NEJM (DESTINY-Breast04/NCT03734029, PFS HR 0.51 in HR+ cohort) + Curigliano G 2024 NEJM (DESTINY-Breast06/NCT04494425, PFS HR 0.62 in HR+/HER2-low). Internal DL pool.
TDXD_HER2LOW_BC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale. k=2, PI suppressed. Populations differ by prior-therapy context (post-chemo in DB04 vs post-endocrine in DB06); indirectness noted.
TDXD_HER2LOW_BC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool. FDA approved T-DXd for HER2-low mBC based on DESTINY-Breast04 (2022); DESTINY-Breast06 extended the benefit to post-endocrine HR+ cohort (2024). The benchmark reflects the pooled DL effect across both pivotals.

LENACAPAVIR_PREP_REVIEW.html.source: Bekker LG 2024 NEJM (PURPOSE-1/NCT04994509, 0 vs 39 events, RR ~0.02) + Kelley CF 2024 NEJM (PURPOSE-2/NCT04925752, 2 vs 9 events, RR 0.11). Internal DL pool.
LENACAPAVIR_PREP_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool on log-scale; PURPOSE-1 uses a 0.5 continuity correction for its 0-event lenacapavir arm. k=2 so PI suppressed and FE-IVW sensitivity reported alongside.
LENACAPAVIR_PREP_REVIEW.html.method_note: Near-complete protection against HIV acquisition with twice-yearly subcutaneous lenacapavir. Both pivotals stopped early by DSMB for overwhelming efficacy. Pool dominated by PURPOSE-2 because PURPOSE-1's zero-event arm inflates variance.

DUPILUMAB_COPD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Annualized moderate/severe COPD exacerbation rate (dupilumab vs placebo in eosinophilic COPD)
DUPILUMAB_COPD_REVIEW.html.source: Bhatt SP 2023 NEJM (BOREAS/NCT03930732, RR 0.70) + Bhatt SP 2024 NEJM (NOTUS/NCT04456673, RR 0.66). Internal DL pool of 2 replicate pivotal phase 3 RCTs.
DUPILUMAB_COPD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects rate-ratio pool on log-scale across 2 pivotal dupilumab-COPD trials. k=2 so PI suppressed and FE-IVW sensitivity reported alongside.
DUPILUMAB_COPD_REVIEW.html.method_note: First biologic to reduce COPD exacerbations. Pool restricted to blood-eosinophil >=300/uL phenotype; not generalisable to non-eosinophilic COPD.

MIRIKIZUMAB_UC_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Clinical remission (modified Mayo) at trial-specific primary timepoint (wk 12 induction; wk 40 maintenance)
MIRIKIZUMAB_UC_REVIEW.html.source: DHaens G 2023 NEJM: LUCENT-1 induction (NCT03518086, OR 2.08) + LUCENT-2 maintenance (NCT03524092, OR 2.97). Internal DL pool.
MIRIKIZUMAB_UC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects pool computed from 2x2 event counts; app's native pooler returns RR scale. Published per-trial adjusted ORs (LUCENT-1 2.08, LUCENT-2 2.97) are larger than corresponding RRs because UC clinical-remission event rates are moderate (13-50%). k=2 so PI suppressed and FE-IVW sensitivity reported alongside. Users can click OR scale on the effect-measure toggle to see the published-OR pool.
MIRIKIZUMAB_UC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Induction and maintenance are different clinical phases and populations (LUCENT-2 re-randomized LUCENT-1 responders); pool treats the class-level IL-23 UC efficacy surface. Per-phase inference preferred for clinical decision-making.

PEGCETACOPLAN_GA_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Change from baseline in GA lesion area (square-root-transformed mm^2) at month 12 (pegcetacoplan monthly vs sham, MD)
PEGCETACOPLAN_GA_REVIEW.html.source: Liao DS et al. Lancet 2023;402:1591-1604 (OAKS 24-month combined analysis + DERBY 24-month): OAKS (NCT03525613, ETD -0.47) + DERBY (NCT03525600, ETD -0.29 at 12 mo, -0.36 at 24 mo). Internal DL pool.
PEGCETACOPLAN_GA_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on sqrt-transformed GA lesion area. k=2 so PI suppressed and FE-IVW sensitivity reported alongside.
PEGCETACOPLAN_GA_REVIEW.html.method_note: DERBY 12-month primary endpoint not met (P=0.086); 24-month combined analysis meets significance. Pool reported at 12 months; sensitivity analysis at 24 months. Safety signal: ~12% exudative AMD conversion on pegcetacoplan vs 3% sham.
PEGCETACOPLAN_GA_REVIEW.html.surrogate_note: GA lesion area on fundus autofluorescence is a regulatory-approved anatomic surrogate; clinical visual-acuity benefit not demonstrated in the 12-month pivotal primary. GRADE indirectness downgrade for the surrogate -> visual-function step.

JAK_RA_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: ACR20 response at week 12 in MTX-IR rheumatoid arthritis
JAK_RA_REVIEW.html.source: Taylor PC 2017 NEJM (RA-BEAM baricitinib/NCT01710358, OR 3.50) + Burmester GR 2018 Lancet (SELECT-NEXT upadacitinib/NCT02675426, OR 3.30) + Combe B 2021 ARD (FINCH-1 filgotinib/NCT02889796, OR 3.28). Internal DL pool across 3 pivotal JAK-class phase 3 RCTs.
JAK_RA_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool on log-risk-ratio scale for ACR20 at week 12 across 3 JAKi-vs-placebo phase 3 RCTs (with MTX background in all arms), computed from 2x2 event counts in the realData block. Published per-trial OR values (3.50, 3.30, 3.28 from logistic regression) are larger than corresponding RRs (1.75, 1.78, 1.56) because ACR20 event rates are high (40-78%).
JAK_RA_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool on RR scale (app's native pooler). The published-literature benchmark is expressed as adjusted OR from logistic regression (class-level OR approximately 3.3 per Singh JA 2022 ACR-class NMA) -- that uses a different effect measure and pooling method. The RR scale is the raw 2x2 count ratio. Both effect measures are valid; ACR20 high event rates make OR > RR.
JAK_RA_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

ANIFROLUMAB_SLE_REVIEW.html.source: Furie R 2019 Lancet Rheumatol (TULIP-1/NCT02446912, BICLA OR 1.55; SRI-4 primary NOT met) + Morand EF 2020 NEJM (TULIP-2/NCT02446899, BICLA OR 1.99). Internal DL pool.
ANIFROLUMAB_SLE_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool on log-risk-ratio scale for BICLA response across 2 pivotal TULIP phase 3 RCTs, computed from 2x2 event counts. k=2, PI suppressed. Published per-trial adjusted OR values 1.55 (TULIP-1) + 1.99 (TULIP-2) are larger than the corresponding RRs because BICLA event rates are 30-68%.
ANIFROLUMAB_SLE_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool on RR scale (app's native pooler). Published literature reports adjusted OR from logistic regression (pooled ~1.8); the RR vs OR difference reflects effect-measure choice, not data disagreement. TULIP-1 SRI-4 primary was not met; BICLA became the programme-level primary for TULIP-2 with transparent outcome-switching reporting. RoB D4 SOME flag for TULIP-1 accordingly.

INSULIN_ICODEC_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: HbA1c change from baseline at the trial-specific primary timepoint (week 26 or 52)
INSULIN_ICODEC_REVIEW.html.source: Rosenstock J 2023 NEJM (ONWARDS-1 icodec vs glargine in insulin-naive T2D/NCT04460885, ETD -0.19) + Philis-Tsimikas A 2023 Lancet D+E (ONWARDS-2 icodec vs degludec basal-switch/NCT04770532, ETD -0.22) + Lingvay I 2023 JAMA (ONWARDS-3 icodec vs degludec insulin-naive T2D/NCT04795531, ETD -0.21). Internal DL pool.
INSULIN_ICODEC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool for HbA1c ETD across 3 ONWARDS T2D phase 3 RCTs
INSULIN_ICODEC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool. Non-inferiority (primary regulatory claim) clearly met; superiority margin (ETD ~-0.2%) below typical MCID of 0.3-0.5% for HbA1c. Narrative pooled benchmark (Kumar 2024) consistent with the app pool.

NIRSEVIMAB_INFANT_RSV_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Medically attended RSV-LRTI through Day 150 (Phase 2b / MELODY) OR RSV-LRTI hospitalisation through first RSV season (HARMONIE)
NIRSEVIMAB_INFANT_RSV_REVIEW.html.source: Griffin MP 2020 NEJM (Nirsevimab Phase 2b preterm/NCT02878330, RR 0.30) + Hammitt LL 2022 NEJM (MELODY late-preterm/term/NCT03979313, RR 0.25) + Drysdale SB 2024 NEJM (HARMONIE pragmatic effectiveness/NCT05110261, RR 0.17). Internal DL pool of 3 pivotal nirsevimab RCTs.
NIRSEVIMAB_INFANT_RSV_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool on log-RR scale across 3 pivotal phase 2b/3 RCTs of nirsevimab vs placebo or no intervention in infants. Primary endpoint definition differs across trials (medically-attended RSV-LRTI vs RSV-hospitalisation); pool interprets the broader class-level protection against RSV medical-care utilisation.
NIRSEVIMAB_INFANT_RSV_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool corresponding to pooled VE approximately 76%. CDC/ACIP 2023 cites consistent 70-80% VE across the nirsevimab pivotal programme.
NIRSEVIMAB_INFANT_RSV_REVIEW.html.timepoint_harmonisation_note: Day 150 is the harmonised timepoint for Phase 2b + MELODY; HARMONIE uses first full RSV season (~Day 150-210) depending on hemisphere.

CAB_PREP_HIV_REVIEW.html.source: Landovitz RJ 2021 NEJM (HPTN 083/NCT02720094, HR 0.34) + Delany-Moretlwe S 2022 Lancet (HPTN 084/NCT03164564, HR 0.12). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 2b/3 RCTs.
CAB_PREP_HIV_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale. k=2; PI suppressed.
CAB_PREP_HIV_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; WHO 2023 PrEP guidelines cite pooled HR approximately 0.2 across HPTN 083 + 084.

COVID_ORAL_ANTIVIRALS_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: COVID-19-related hospitalisation or death through Day 28-29
COVID_ORAL_ANTIVIRALS_REVIEW.html.source: Hammond J 2022 NEJM (EPIC-HR Paxlovid/NCT04960202, RR 0.11) + Jayk Bernal A 2022 NEJM (MOVe-OUT molnupiravir/NCT04575597, RR 0.69). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 RCTs.
COVID_ORAL_ANTIVIRALS_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool on log-scale. Class-level pool with substantial effect heterogeneity (nirmatrelvir 89% vs molnupiravir 31% relative reduction).
COVID_ORAL_ANTIVIRALS_REVIEW.html.method_note: Class-level pool; pre-specified GRADE indirectness downgrade given mechanistic differences between the two drugs. Drug-specific pools are the preferred inference surface.
COVID_ORAL_ANTIVIRALS_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

VENETOCLAX_AML_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Overall survival in untreated AML ineligible for intensive chemo
VENETOCLAX_AML_REVIEW.html.source: DiNardo CD 2020 NEJM (VIALE-A venetoclax + azacitidine/NCT02993523, HR 0.66) + Wei AH 2020 Blood (VIALE-C venetoclax + LDAC/NCT03069352, updated HR 0.70). Internal DL pool.
VENETOCLAX_AML_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale across 2 VIALE phase 3 OS RCTs. VIALE-C used the 6-month updated analysis; initial primary did not meet significance. k=2, PI suppressed.
VENETOCLAX_AML_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool. VIALE-C estimand distinction flagged in the risk-of-bias assessment. VIALE-A was the definitive HMA-backbone trial; VIALE-C LDAC backbone.

CART_DLBCL_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Event-free survival in 2L R/R aggressive B-cell lymphoma
CART_DLBCL_REVIEW.html.source: Locke FL 2022 NEJM (ZUMA-7 axi-cel/NCT03391466, HR 0.40) + Kamdar M 2022 Lancet (TRANSFORM liso-cel/NCT03575351, HR 0.35) + Bishop MR 2022 NEJM (BELINDA tisa-cel/NCT03570892, HR 1.07 NEGATIVE). Internal DL pool.
CART_DLBCL_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale across 3 pivotal 2L CAR-T-vs-SoC RCTs. Very high heterogeneity expected (ZUMA-7 + TRANSFORM strongly positive; BELINDA negative due to 52-day manufacturing time + 26% bridging therapy).
CART_DLBCL_REVIEW.html.method_note: Class-level pool with pre-specified indirectness downgrade in GRADE. Product-specific inference is the preferred clinical surface. BELINDA's null result reflects manufacturing/bridging disadvantage, not a true absence of effect.
CART_DLBCL_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

IL23_PSORIASIS_REVIEW.html.source: Internal DL random-effects pool of Blauvelt A 2017 JAAD (VOYAGE 1/NCT02207231, guselkumab, published OR 25.80) + Gordon KB 2018 Lancet (UltIMMa-1/NCT02684370, risankizumab, published OR 59.21) + Reich K 2017 Lancet (reSURFACE 1/NCT01722331, tildrakizumab, published OR 48.38).
IL23_PSORIASIS_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects OR pool of PASI 90 at the trial-specific pre-specified primary timepoint across 3 phase 3 IL-23 inhibitor vs placebo RCTs. Pool on the log-odds scale back-transformed to OR. High heterogeneity expected across agents (guselkumab vs risankizumab vs tildrakizumab) and timepoints (wk12 vs wk16).
IL23_PSORIASIS_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool on log-odds scale (each trial's OR is huge because placebo arms had near-zero PASI 90 events); the linear-space arithmetic mean of the three agent-specific ORs (~44) overstates the random-effects pool because the large trials with larger denominators dominate the inverse-variance weighting. The log-odds DL pool (OR ~21) is the mathematically correct class-level estimate.
IL23_PSORIASIS_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across distinct active agents; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious' unless within-agent subgroup confirms homogeneity

KARXT_SCZ_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: PANSS total change from baseline at week 5
KARXT_SCZ_REVIEW.html.source: Kaul I 2024 Lancet (EMERGENT-2/NCT04659161, MD -9.6) + Kaul I 2024 JAMA Psychiatry (EMERGENT-3/NCT04738123, MD -8.4). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 RCTs.
KARXT_SCZ_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on PANSS total change. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 approaches zero (Q < k-1 under normal approximation); FE-IVW sensitivity equivalent.
KARXT_SCZ_REVIEW.html.method_note: Internal DL pool; EMERGENT-2 and EMERGENT-3 were independent replicate pivotal trials with concordant point estimates. First muscarinic (M1/M4) antipsychotic; distinct from D2 antagonist class.

AFLIBERCEPT_HD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: BCVA letter-score change at week 48 (non-inferiority, 8 mg Q12W minus 2 mg Q8W)
AFLIBERCEPT_HD_REVIEW.html.source: Lanzetta P 2024 Lancet (PULSAR nAMD/NCT04964089, ETD +1.0) + Brown DM 2024 Lancet (PHOTON DME/NCT04429503, ETD +0.5). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 non-inferiority RCTs.
AFLIBERCEPT_HD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on BCVA letter scale. Q<k-1 -> tau2=0; FE-IVW sensitivity equivalent. Primary comparison is 8 mg Q12W vs 2 mg Q8W (Q16W arm is secondary).
AFLIBERCEPT_HD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Class-level pool across distinct indications (nAMD vs DME); pre-specified GRADE indirectness downgrade. Both trials met the pre-specified -4-letter non-inferiority margin.
AFLIBERCEPT_HD_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: pool across distinct indications (nAMD and DME) for the same drug/dose; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious'

PATISIRAN_POLYNEUROPATHY_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: mNIS+7 change from baseline at month 18 (siRNA minus placebo)
PATISIRAN_POLYNEUROPATHY_REVIEW.html.source: Adams D 2018 NEJM (APOLLO patisiran/NCT01960348, LS mean diff -34.0) + Adams D 2023 Amyloid (HELIOS-A vutrisiran 18-mo/NCT03759379, LS mean diff -28.55 vs external APOLLO placebo). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 siRNA RCTs.
PATISIRAN_POLYNEUROPATHY_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on mNIS+7 scale. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 ~= 5.5; HKSJ CI applied. k=2, PI suppressed.
PATISIRAN_POLYNEUROPATHY_REVIEW.html.method_note: Class-level TTR-silencing siRNA pool. HELIOS-A primary comparator is the external APOLLO placebo arm (prospective design); partial placebo overlap is a known methodological limitation, pre-specified GRADE downgrade for comparator heterogeneity.
PATISIRAN_POLYNEUROPATHY_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: HELIOS-A uses external APOLLO placebo arm (prospective-external design); GRADE indirectness + RoB D2 'some concerns' pre-specified

PBC_PPAR_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Biochemical response at week 52 / month 12 (composite: ALP <1.67x ULN with >=15% reduction AND total bilirubin <=ULN)
PBC_PPAR_REVIEW.html.source: Kowdley KV 2024 NEJM (ELATIVE elafibranor/NCT04526665, RR 13.4) + Hirschfield GM 2024 NEJM (RESPONSE seladelpar/NCT04620240, RR 3.00). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 PPAR modulator PBC-2L RCTs.
PBC_PPAR_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects RR pool on log-RR scale across 2 phase 3 PBC second-line PPAR modulator RCTs. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 ~= 0.84; HKSJ CI applied. High heterogeneity (RR 13.4 vs 3.0) reflects different placebo response rates (4% ELATIVE vs 20% RESPONSE) and different PPAR receptor selectivity (elafibranor alpha/delta vs seladelpar selective delta).
PBC_PPAR_REVIEW.html.method_note: Class-level PPAR modulator pool; pre-specified GRADE indirectness downgrade. Drug-specific inference preferred. Wide pooled CI reflects substantial between-trial heterogeneity driven by placebo-arm base rate differences.
PBC_PPAR_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: class-level pool across PPAR alpha/delta and selective delta agonists; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious'

LU_PSMA_MCRPC_REVIEW.html.source: Sartor O 2021 NEJM (VISION post-taxane/NCT03511664, rPFS HR 0.40) + Morris MJ 2024 Lancet (PSMAfore taxane-naive/NCT04689828, rPFS HR 0.43). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 177Lu-PSMA-617 radioligand therapy RCTs.
LU_PSMA_MCRPC_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale across 2 pivotal 177Lu-PSMA-617 vs active-comparator mCRPC rPFS RCTs. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 ~= 0; FE-IVW sensitivity equivalent.
LU_PSMA_MCRPC_REVIEW.html.method_note: Pool across distinct lines (post-taxane VISION vs taxane-naive PSMAfore) and distinct comparators (SoC with ARPI + chemo/bone-targeted in VISION vs ARPI switch in PSMAfore); pre-specified GRADE indirectness downgrade. Both trials BICR-adjudicated rPFS; OS interpretation confounded by high crossover in PSMAfore.
LU_PSMA_MCRPC_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: pool across distinct treatment lines (post-taxane vs pre-taxane) and heterogeneous comparators; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious'

SOTAGLIFLOZIN_HF_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: Total CV death, HF hospitalisation, or urgent HF visit (amended-primary after truncation)
SOTAGLIFLOZIN_HF_REVIEW.html.source: Bhatt DL 2021 NEJM (SOLOIST-WHF T2D+acute HF/NCT03521934, HR 0.67) + Bhatt DL 2021 NEJM (SCORED T2D+CKD/NCT03315143, HR 0.74). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 sotagliflozin dual SGLT1/2 CV-outcomes RCTs.
SOTAGLIFLOZIN_HF_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale across 2 pivotal sotagliflozin CV-composite RCTs. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 ~= 0; FE-IVW sensitivity equivalent.
SOTAGLIFLOZIN_HF_REVIEW.html.method_note: Same-drug pool but across distinct T2D populations (SOLOIST-WHF post-HF-hospitalisation vs SCORED CKD with CV risk factors). Both trials truncated early due to sponsor funding loss; primary endpoint amended during truncation (total events vs time-to-first). Dual SGLT1/2 mechanism is distinct from selective SGLT2i class (SGLT2_HF separate app).

ESKETAMINE_TRD_REVIEW.html.primary_outcome: MADRS total score change from baseline at day 28
ESKETAMINE_TRD_REVIEW.html.source: Popova V 2019 Am J Psychiatry (TRANSFORM-2 age 18-64/NCT02418585, MD -4.0) + Ochs-Ross R 2020 Am J Geriatr Psychiatry (TRANSFORM-3 age >=65/NCT02422186, MD -3.6). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 esketamine-vs-placebo TRD induction RCTs with concordant MADRS day-28 primary endpoint.
ESKETAMINE_TRD_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects MD pool on MADRS scale. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 ~= 0 (Q much less than k-1); FE-IVW sensitivity equivalent.
ESKETAMINE_TRD_REVIEW.html.method_note: Same-drug pool; age subgroup (18-64 vs >=65) is a pre-specified subgroup, not an indirectness threat. TRANSFORM-1 (flexible 56/84 mg, age 18-64) did not meet MMRM primary (MD -3.2, 95% CI -6.88 to +0.45) but is directionally concordant; excluded from primary pool under pre-specified criteria and reported as sensitivity.

ACALABRUTINIB_CLL_REVIEW.html.source: Sharman JP 2022 Leukemia (ELEVATE-TN 58-mo update/NCT02475681, acala-mono vs Clb+Obi HR 0.21) + Jurczak W 2023 Am J Hematol (ASCEND 46-mo update/NCT02970318, acala vs investigator choice HR 0.28). Internal DL pool of 2 pivotal phase 3 BTKi RCTs.
ACALABRUTINIB_CLL_REVIEW.html.method: DL random-effects HR pool on log-hazard scale. Fisher-scoring REML tau2 small (0.007); HKSJ CI applied. k=2, PI suppressed.
ACALABRUTINIB_CLL_REVIEW.html.method_note: Pool across distinct lines (treatment-naive ELEVATE-TN vs R/R ASCEND) and distinct comparators (Clb+Obi chemoimmunotherapy vs investigator choice including idelalisib+R or BR); pre-specified GRADE indirectness downgrade. Class-level BTKi effect estimand.
ACALABRUTINIB_CLL_REVIEW.html.indirectness_note: pool across distinct treatment lines (TN vs R/R) and heterogeneous comparators; GRADE indirectness pre-specified as 'serious'
